T1550.001 - Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token
cwe: CWE-74 - Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in Output Used by a Downstream Component

threat.title: gRPC Metadata Injection Leading to Authorization Bypass and Privilege Escalation
threat.description: Attackers exploit insufficient validation of gRPC metadata (custom HTTP/2 headers) to inject malicious headers that bypass authentication interceptors, escalate privileges, or poison HPACK compression tables. Since gRPC metadata is transmitted as HTTP/2 headers and can be manipulated by clients, unvalidated metadata enables attackers to forge authorization tokens, modify user roles, bypass rate limiting, or cause denial of service through header flooding attacks.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker analyzes gRPC service using grpcurl or grpcui to identify metadata keys used for authorization
2. Discovers that interceptor checks 'authorization' metadata but fails to validate 'x-user-role' custom metadata
3. Crafts malicious gRPC request with injected metadata: metadata.set('x-user-role', 'admin')
4. Bypasses authentication interceptor which only validates JWT in 'authorization' header
5. Backend service trusts 'x-user-role' metadata without verification, granting admin privileges
6. Attacker executes privileged operations: deletes resources, exfiltrates sensitive data, modifies system configuration
prerequisites: gRPC service accepts custom metadata without strict validation
Authorization logic trusts client-provided metadata fields
Missing or incomplete interceptor chain validation
No metadata whitelist or sanitization implemented
Backend services trust metadata forwarded from proxies

cve_references.description: gRPC HTTP2 stack header size exceeded error exposes sensitive information through malicious metadata headers
OpenTelemetry Collector gRPC receiver DoS via compressed bomb in metadata (zstd compression bypass)
grpc-js vulnerable to metadata manipulation allowing authentication bypass
RustFS gRPC hardcoded token authentication bypass through metadata injection

real_world_breaches.incident: Google GKE Metadata Service Privilege Escalation
real_world_breaches.impact: Privilege escalation via GKE metadata protection downgrade attack allowing container escape and cluster admin access
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Insufficient validation of metadata service requests in GKE allowing MITM attacks on instance metadata

real_world_breaches.impact: Clients communicating through HTTP/2 proxy experienced failed requests and leaked HTTP header keys due to HPACK table corruption
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Malicious gRPC client poisoned HPACK compression table between proxy and backend services

real_world_breaches.impact: Multiple gRPC header parsing vulnerabilities exposing sensitive data and enabling DoS attacks
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Insufficient validation of HTTP/2 headers in gRPC implementation

security_research.key_finding: Many production gRPC services lack proper metadata validation, enabling header injection attacks that bypass authentication

mitigations.title: Implement Strict Metadata Validation with Whitelist
mitigations.description: Validate all incoming gRPC metadata against a strict whitelist of allowed keys and value patterns. Reject requests with unexpected metadata fields before they reach business logic. Use regex patterns to enforce metadata value formats and implement length limits to prevent buffer overflow attacks.
validation_steps: Create test cases with malicious metadata keys (e.g., 'x-admin', 'x-role')
Verify requests with non-whitelisted metadata are rejected with InvalidArgument
Test metadata values exceeding length limits
Confirm regex patterns correctly validate authorization tokens
Use grpcurl to send crafted metadata: grpcurl -H 'x-malicious: value' ...

mitigations.title: Implement Context-Aware Authorization in Interceptors
mitigations.description: Never trust client-provided metadata for authorization decisions. Extract and validate authentication tokens (JWT, OAuth2) from metadata, then perform server-side authorization checks against a trusted identity provider or database. Ensure interceptor chain validates tokens before any business logic executes.
nist_csf: PR.AC-1 - Identities and credentials are managed
PR.PT-3 - Access to systems and assets is controlled
validation_steps: Test with valid JWT token - should succeed
Test with expired JWT token - should return UNAUTHENTICATED
Test with malformed token - should return UNAUTHENTICATED
Test with metadata injection (e.g., x-user-role: admin) - should be ignored
Verify server-side permissions are checked, not client metadata

mitigations.title: Sanitize Metadata in gRPC-Gateway and Proxies
mitigations.description: When using grpc-gateway or proxies (Envoy, Nginx) that convert HTTP to gRPC, sanitize incoming HTTP headers before converting to metadata. Implement header filtering to remove potentially malicious headers and enforce strict content-type validation.

code_example.code:
import grpc
import re
from typing import Callable, Any, Tuple, List, Set

class MetadataSanitizerInterceptor(grpc.ServerInterceptor):
    """
    gRPC server interceptor that sanitizes incoming metadata
    to prevent header injection attacks.
    """
    
    def __init__(self):
        # Define allowed metadata keys (whitelist)
        self.allowed_keys: Set[str] = {
            'authorization',
            'content-type',
            'user-agent',
            'x-request-id',
            'x-b3-traceid',
            'x-b3-spanid',
            'x-b3-sampled'
        }
        
        # Define forbidden patterns (prevent injection)
        self.forbidden_patterns: List[re.Pattern] = [
            re.compile(r'x-(admin|role|permission|privilege)', re.IGNORECASE),
            re.compile(r'x-forwarded-(for|proto|host)', re.IGNORECASE),  # Prevent proxy header injection
            re.compile(r'\r|\n'),  # Prevent CRLF injection
            re.compile(r'[\x00-\x1F\x7F]'),  # Control characters
        ]
        
        # Maximum metadata size (prevent DoS)
        self.max_metadata_size = 8192  # 8KB limit
        self.max_value_length = 2048   # 2KB per value

    def sanitize_metadata(self, metadata: Tuple[Tuple[str, str], ...]) -> List[Tuple[str, str]]:
        """
        Sanitize incoming metadata by filtering and validating.
        """
        sanitized = []
        total_size = 0
        
        for key, value in metadata:
            # Convert to lowercase for comparison
            key_lower = key.lower()
            
            # Skip gRPC internal metadata
            if key_lower.startswith('grpc-'):
                continue
            
            # Check if key is whitelisted
            if key_lower not in self.allowed_keys:
                # Log suspicious metadata (but don't include values)
                print(f"[SECURITY] Rejected non-whitelisted metadata key: {key_lower}")
                continue
            
            # Check for forbidden patterns in key and value
            if any(pattern.search(key) for pattern in self.forbidden_patterns):
                print(f"[SECURITY] Rejected metadata with forbidden pattern in key: {key}")
                continue
            
            if any(pattern.search(value) for pattern in self.forbidden_patterns):
                print(f"[SECURITY] Rejected metadata with forbidden pattern in value for key: {key}")
                continue
            
            # Check value length
            if len(value) > self.max_value_length:
                print(f"[SECURITY] Rejected metadata value exceeding length limit: {key}")
                continue
            
            # Check total metadata size
            total_size += len(key) + len(value)
            if total_size > self.max_metadata_size:
                print(f"[SECURITY] Rejected metadata exceeding total size limit")
                break
            
            # Value passed all checks
            sanitized.append((key, value))
        
        return sanitized

    def intercept_service(self, continuation: Callable, handler_call_details: grpc.HandlerCallDetails) -> grpc.RpcMethodHandler:
        """
        Intercept incoming RPC calls to sanitize metadata.
        """
        # Extract and sanitize metadata
        original_metadata = handler_call_details.invocation_metadata
        sanitized_metadata = self.sanitize_metadata(original_metadata)
        
        # Create new handler call details with sanitized metadata
        sanitized_call_details = grpc.HandlerCallDetails(
            method=handler_call_details.method,
            invocation_metadata=tuple(sanitized_metadata)
        )
        
        # Continue to next interceptor/handler
        return continuation(sanitized_call_details)


class AuthenticationInterceptor(grpc.ServerInterceptor):
    """
    Authentication interceptor that validates JWT tokens.
    Must run AFTER metadata sanitization.
    """
    
    def __init__(self, jwt_public_key: str):
        self.jwt_public_key = jwt_public_key
    
    def extract_token(self, metadata: Tuple[Tuple[str, str], ...]) -> str:
        """
        Extract Bearer token from authorization metadata.
        """
        for key, value in metadata:
            if key.lower() == 'authorization':
                if value.startswith('Bearer '):
                    return value[7:]  # Remove 'Bearer ' prefix
        raise grpc.RpcError('Missing or invalid authorization header')
    
    def verify_token(self, token: str) -> dict:
        """
        Verify JWT token (simplified - use PyJWT in production).
        """
        import jwt
        try:
            payload = jwt.decode(
                token,
                self.jwt_public_key,
                algorithms=['RS256'],
                options={
                    'verify_signature': True,
                    'verify_exp': True,
                    'verify_nbf': True,
                    'verify_iat': True,
                    'verify_aud': True
                }
            )
            return payload
        except jwt.InvalidTokenError as e:
            raise grpc.RpcError(f'Token verification failed: {str(e)}')
    
    def intercept_service(self, continuation: Callable, handler_call_details: grpc.HandlerCallDetails) -> grpc.RpcMethodHandler:
        """
        Intercept incoming RPC calls to authenticate.
        """
        try:
            # Extract and verify token
            token = self.extract_token(handler_call_details.invocation_metadata)
            user_claims = self.verify_token(token)
            
            # CRITICAL: Store verified claims in context, NOT from client metadata
            # This prevents metadata injection attacks
            # (Implementation would store claims in server context)
            
            # Continue to handler
            return continuation(handler_call_details)
            
        except Exception as e:
            # Authentication failed - abort RPC
            def abort(request, context):
                context.abort(grpc.StatusCode.UNAUTHENTICATED, str(e))
            
            return grpc.unary_unary_rpc_method_handler(
                abort,
                request_deserializer=lambda x: x,
                response_serializer=lambda x: x
            )


# Usage example
if __name__ == '__main__':
    import time
    
    # Load JWT public key
    with open('jwt-public.pem', 'r') as f:
        jwt_public_key = f.read()
    
    # Create interceptor chain (order matters!)
    interceptors = [
        MetadataSanitizerInterceptor(),      # First: Sanitize
        AuthenticationInterceptor(jwt_public_key),  # Then: Authenticate
    ]
    
    # Create gRPC server with interceptors
    server = grpc.server(
        futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=10),
        interceptors=interceptors,
        options=[
            ('grpc.max_metadata_size', 8192),  # Limit metadata size
            ('grpc.max_receive_message_length', 4 * 1024 * 1024),  # 4MB
        ]
    )
    
    # Add your service implementation
    # your_pb2_grpc.add_YourServiceServicer_to_server(YourServiceImpl(), server)
    
    server.add_insecure_port('[::]:50051')
    server.start()
    print('gRPC server started with metadata sanitization and authentication')
    
    try:
        while True:
            time.sleep(86400)
    except KeyboardInterrupt:
        server.stop(0)

validation_steps: Test with CRLF injection in metadata values
Attempt to inject x-admin-role metadata
Send metadata exceeding size limits
Verify control characters are stripped from values
Confirm only whitelisted keys are forwarded to handlers

mitigations.title: Enable gRPC Metadata Size Limits and Rate Limiting
mitigations.description: Configure HTTP/2 frame size limits, HPACK table size limits, and implement rate limiting on metadata-heavy requests to prevent DoS attacks through header flooding or compression bomb attacks.

code_example.code:
# Envoy proxy configuration for gRPC metadata protection
static_resources:
  listeners:
  - name: grpc_listener
    address:
      socket_address:
        address: 0.0.0.0
        port_value: 9090
    filter_chains:
    - filters:
      - name: envoy.filters.network.http_connection_manager
        typed_config:
          "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.network.http_connection_manager.v3.HttpConnectionManager
          stat_prefix: grpc_ingress
          codec_type: AUTO
          
          # HTTP/2 protocol options for gRPC
          http2_protocol_options:
            # Limit max concurrent streams to prevent resource exhaustion
            max_concurrent_streams: 100
            
            # Initial connection window size (flow control)
            initial_connection_window_size: 1048576  # 1MB
            initial_stream_window_size: 65536        # 64KB
            
            # HPACK header compression settings
            hpack_table_size: 4096  # Limit HPACK dynamic table size
            max_header_list_size: 8192  # 8KB max headers (prevents header bomb)
            
            # Limit frame sizes
            max_frame_size: 16384  # 16KB max frame size
            
            # Override stream error on invalid HTTP messaging
            override_stream_error_on_invalid_http_message: true
          
          # Request timeout settings
          request_timeout: 30s
          stream_idle_timeout: 300s
          
          # Rate limiting configuration
          http_filters:
          - name: envoy.filters.http.ratelimit
            typed_config:
              "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.ratelimit.v3.RateLimit
              domain: grpc_metadata_ratelimit
              enable_x_ratelimit_headers: DRAFT_VERSION_03
              rate_limit_service:
                grpc_service:
                  envoy_grpc:
                    cluster_name: rate_limit_cluster
                  timeout: 0.25s
              
              # Rate limit based on metadata size
              descriptors:
              - entries:
                - key: header_match
                  value: large_metadata
                  descriptors:
                  - key: remote_address
                    rate_limit:
                      unit: MINUTE
                      requests_per_unit: 10
          
          # Header validation and sanitization
          - name: envoy.filters.http.header_mutation
            typed_config:
              "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.header_mutation.v3.HeaderMutation
              mutations:
                # Remove dangerous client-supplied headers
                - remove: x-admin-role
                - remove: x-user-role
                - remove: x-permission
                - remove: x-privilege
                - remove: x-forwarded-for  # Prevent IP spoofing
                - remove: x-real-ip
          
          # gRPC-Web support (if needed)
          - name: envoy.filters.http.grpc_web
            typed_config:
              "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.grpc_web.v3.GrpcWeb
          
          # Router (must be last)
          - name: envoy.filters.http.router
            typed_config:
              "@type": type.googleapis.com/envoy.extensions.filters.http.router.v3.Router
          
          route_config:
            name: grpc_routes
            virtual_hosts:
            - name: grpc_backend
              domains: ["*"]
              routes:
              - match:
                  prefix: "/"
                  grpc: {}  # gRPC route matcher
                route:
                  cluster: grpc_backend_cluster
                  timeout: 30s
                  
                  # Per-route rate limiting
                  rate_limits:
                  - actions:
                    - request_headers:
                        header_name: ":path"
                        descriptor_key: path
                  
                  # Retry policy for transient failures
                  retry_policy:
                    retry_on: "5xx"
                    num_retries: 3
                    per_try_timeout: 10s
  
  clusters:
  - name: grpc_backend_cluster
    connect_timeout: 5s
    type: STRICT_DNS
    lb_policy: ROUND_ROBIN
    http2_protocol_options:
      # Backend HTTP/2 settings
      max_concurrent_streams: 100
      initial_connection_window_size: 1048576
    load_assignment:
      cluster_name: grpc_backend_cluster
      endpoints:
      - lb_endpoints:
        - endpoint:
            address:
              socket_address:
                address: grpc-backend
                port_value: 50051
    
    # Health check for backend
    health_checks:
    - timeout: 5s
      interval: 10s
      unhealthy_threshold: 3
      healthy_threshold: 2
      grpc_health_check:
        service_name: "grpc.health.v1.Health"
  
  # Rate limit service cluster
  - name: rate_limit_cluster
    connect_timeout: 1s
    type: STRICT_DNS
    lb_policy: ROUND_ROBIN
    http2_protocol_options: {}
    load_assignment:
      cluster_name: rate_limit_cluster
      endpoints:
      - lb_endpoints:
        - endpoint:
            address:
              socket_address:
                address: ratelimit-service
                port_value: 8081

admin:
  address:
    socket_address:
      address: 0.0.0.0
      port_value: 9901

nist_csf: PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
DE.CM-1 - The network is monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events
validation_steps: Test with oversized metadata headers (should be rejected)
Send requests with HPACK compression table overflow
Verify rate limiting triggers after threshold
Confirm malicious headers (x-admin-role) are removed
Monitor Envoy admin interface for header size violations

indicators: Unusual metadata keys in gRPC requests (e.g., x-admin, x-role, x-permission)
Authorization bypass attempts with low authentication failure rate but high privilege access
Requests with metadata size exceeding normal thresholds (>4KB)
HPACK table poisoning causing failed requests across multiple clients
Repeated requests with slightly varied metadata keys (enumeration)
gRPC status code distribution skewed toward PERMISSION_DENIED or UNAUTHENTICATED
Metadata containing CRLF sequences or control characters

log_queries.query:
// Detect gRPC metadata injection attempts
let suspiciousMetadataKeys = dynamic(['x-admin', 'x-role', 'x-permission', 'x-privilege', 'x-user-role']);
AppServiceHTTPLogs
| where Protocol contains "h2" or Protocol contains "grpc"
| extend MetadataKeys = extract_all(@'"([^"]+)":', dynamic([1]), RequestHeader)
| where MetadataKeys has_any (suspiciousMetadataKeys)
| extend ClientIP = tostring(split(CIp, ':')[0])
| summarize 
    AttemptCount = count(),
    UniqueMetadataKeys = make_set(MetadataKeys),
    UniqueEndpoints = dcount(CsUriStem),
    StatusCodes = make_set(ScStatus)
    by ClientIP, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where AttemptCount > 5
| extend Severity = case(
    StatusCodes has 200, "Critical",  // Successful with suspicious metadata
    StatusCodes has 403 or StatusCodes has 401, "High",
    "Medium"
  )
| project TimeGenerated, ClientIP, AttemptCount, UniqueMetadataKeys, UniqueEndpoints, StatusCodes, Severity
| order by AttemptCount desc

index=grpc_logs sourcetype=grpc:server
| rex field=metadata "(?<metadata_keys>[x-admin|x-role|x-permission|x-privilege|x-user-role])"
| where isnotnull(metadata_keys)
| eval metadata_size = len(metadata)
| eval is_large_metadata = if(metadata_size > 4096, 1, 0)
| stats 
    count as request_count,
    dc(method_path) as unique_methods,
    values(metadata_keys) as injected_keys,
    avg(metadata_size) as avg_metadata_size,
    max(metadata_size) as max_metadata_size,
    sum(is_large_metadata) as large_metadata_count,
    values(status_code) as status_codes
    by client_ip, _time span=5m
| where request_count > 10 OR large_metadata_count > 3
| eval severity = case(
    match(status_codes, "OK|0") AND large_metadata_count > 0, "critical",
    match(status_codes, "PERMISSION_DENIED|UNAUTHENTICATED"), "high",
    1=1, "medium"
  )
| table _time, client_ip, request_count, unique_methods, injected_keys, avg_metadata_size, max_metadata_size, status_codes, severity
| sort -request_count

grpc.metadata.keys:(x-admin OR x-role OR x-permission OR x-privilege OR x-user-role) 
AND (
  grpc.status:(OK OR PERMISSION_DENIED OR UNAUTHENTICATED) OR
  grpc.metadata.size:>4096
)
| stats 
    count() as attempt_count,
    cardinality(grpc.method_path) as unique_methods,
    values(grpc.metadata.keys) as suspicious_keys,
    avg(grpc.metadata.size) as avg_size,
    max(grpc.metadata.size) as max_size
  by source.ip, timebucket(5m)
| where attempt_count > 5

fields @timestamp, @message
| filter @message like /grpc/ and @message like /(x-admin|x-role|x-permission|x-privilege)/
| parse @message /metadata\=(?<metadata>.*?)(\s|$)/
| stats 
    count() as request_count,
    count_distinct(method_path) as unique_methods
  by client_ip, bin(5m) as time_window
| filter request_count > 10
| sort request_count desc
